56.reason: [When the stack is empty, the pop() function does not return a value, leading to undefined behavior when the caller tries to use the return value. This causes incorrect output or runtime errors.]

72.reason: [If pop() prints "error " due to an empty stack, it doesn't return a valid integer, so 'ans' gets an undefined value, which is then printed as garbage instead of handling the error properly.]
